chunks.text: February 7, 2024: Ion Stoica elected to the National Academy of Engineering. EECS Professor Ion Stoica has been elected to the National Academy of Engineering. Stoica was honored 'for networked systems for large-scale data processing, analytics, and machine learning.' He joins 114 newly elected members, as well as 21 international members to be inducted into the NAE, which is among the highest professional distinctions accorded to an engineer.

February 5, 2024: Amy Wendt Wins CITRIS Athena Award for Academic Leadership. EE alumna Amy Wendt (M.S. '85, Ph.D. '88) has won the 2024 EDGE in Tech Athena Award for Academic Leadership. The Center for Information Technology Research in the Interest of Society and the Banatao Institute (CITRIS) confers four categories of awards to exceptional pioneers in diversity, equity, and inclusion in technology.

February 1, 2024: EECS alum develops tongue-controlled touchpad. EECS alumnus Corten Singer (B.A.'17 CS, B.A.'17 CogSci, M.S.'18 EECS), co-founder of Augmental, an assistive technologies company, has developed MouthPad^ the world's first hands-free touchpad. MouthPad^ debuted at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show.

January 30, 2024: CS alumna Xiaoye Sherry Lee is elected Vice President-At-Large of SIAM. CS alumna Xiaoye Sherry Li (Ph.D. '96; Advisor: James Demmel) has been elected as Vice President-At-Large of the Society of Industrial and Applied Mathematics (SIAM). Li's two-year term began on January 1, 2024. Li was named a SIAM Fellow in 2016 and has since served in various leadership roles.

December 14, 2023: CS alumna Giulia Guidi wins SIAM Activity Group on Supercomputing Early Career Prize. CS alumna Giulia Guidi (Ph.D. '22; Advisors: Aydın Buluç and Katherine Yelick) has won the SIAM Activity Group on Supercomputing Early Career Prize for her work in applying High-performance computing to computational genomics. Guidi is now an assistant professor in the Department of Computer Science and a Graduate Field Faculty at Cornell University.

November 21, 2023: TIME 100 most influential people in AI list includes CDSS community members. This entry notes that members from the CDSS community were included in the TIME 100 AI list, highlighting their contributions and influence in the field of artificial intelligence.

December 14, 2023: Theodore 'Ted' Van Duzer has died. EECS Professor Emeritus Theodore 'Ted' Van Duzer passed away peacefully in his sleep on October 24th. He was 95. Ted was born in Piscataway Township, New Jersey in 1927. At 17, he joined the Navy as a radio technician, his entrée into a career in electrical engineering. With assistance from the GI Bill, he earned his degrees and later became a professor at Berkeley.

September 9, 2023: Help Us Honor a Forgotten Hero. An effort is underway to restore the legacy of Berkeley EE Prof. Joseph Gier to the campus community. Gier, who was the first African American professor to earn tenure at the University of California, taught and ran a lab in Cory Hall between 1939 and 1958. The initiative aims to recognize his contributions and historical significance.

July 27, 2023: NASA astronaut Warren 'Woody' Hoburg interviewed on The Robot Brains Podcast. NASA astronaut and EECS alumnus Warren 'Woody' Hoburg (M.S.'11, Ph.D.'13 EECS) was interviewed by The Robot Brains Podcast while aboard the International Space Station (ISS). CS Professor Pieter Abbeel, who is the brains behind the podcast as well as Woody's Ph.D. advisor, interviewed Woody about life on the ISS, his research, and experiences in space.

June 28, 2023: New Gen Z graduates are fluent in AI and ready to join the workforce. Edith Llontop (EECS B.S. '23) quoted in The Washington Post. This article discusses how recent graduates from Generation Z are skilled in artificial intelligence and prepared for employment, with Edith Llontop as an example cited in a major publication.
